steps.title: Identify the two-part requirement
steps.content: The question asks which choice “most effectively uses information from the given sentences to present the study and its findings”—meaning an effective answer must name the researchers/study AND state the actual findings. This dual requirement is the key criterion.

steps.title: Match both parts to the choices
steps.content:
Choice B does this completely: it names the researchers conducting the study, then provides the main finding (146 total springtail species) plus the specific breakdown by genus (1 from Hemisotoma, 3 from Sphaeridia).

Choice A is incomplete (sentence cuts off), Choice C describes what the study aimed to find but never states the actual findings, and Choice D omits the researchers and study context entirely.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
